etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 奐(奐) (huàn)
etymology.interpretation: The character 换(換) uses the semantic part 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") to signify an action typically performed by the hand, such as exchanging or swapping. The phonetic 奐(奐) (huàn) provides the pronunciation, and together they indicate the act of using hands to make an exchange.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 換 switched from 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") to 扌(扌), the simplified hand radical, and retained 奐(奐) (huàn) for phonetic purpose.

examples.chinese: 请帮我换桌子(請幫我換桌子)。
examples.english: Please help me exchange the table.

examples.chinese: 如果你不喜欢这件衣服(如果你不喜歡這件衣服)，可以去店里换另一件(可以去店裡換另一件)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ bù xǐhuān zhè jiàn yīfu, kěyǐ qù diàn lǐ huàn lìng yī jiàn.
examples.english: If you don't like this piece of clothing, you can go to the store to exchange it for another.

examples.chinese: 我每两个月换一次手机壳(我每兩個月換一次手機殼)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měi liǎng gè yuè huàn yīcì shǒujī ké.
examples.english: I change my phone case every two months.
